LanguageData.Drac_bench_smithy.label: draconic smithy
LanguageData.Drac_bench_smithy.description: A rugged smithy used by Dracaena colonists for heavy metalwork.
LanguageData.Drac_bench_stove.label: draconic cookstove
LanguageData.Drac_bench_stove.description: A broad cookstove used for kneading and baking grain-based food.
LanguageData.Drac_bench_tailor.label: draconic tailoring bench
LanguageData.Drac_bench_tailor.description: A tailoring bench adapted for Dracaena apparel and handcraft work.
LanguageData.Drac_bench_herb.label: draconic agronomy bench
LanguageData.Drac_bench_herb.description: A processing bench for herbal materials and grain preparation.
LanguageData.DO_Plant_Wheat_Desc: A staple grain crop cultivated by Dracaena settlers. It needs fertile soil and time, but supports a full milling and baking chain.
LanguageData.DO_WheatEar_Desc: Harvested wheat ears. Basic grain material for porridge and flour.
LanguageData.DO_Flour_Desc: Ground wheat flour. Better digestibility than raw grain and the core input for dough and yeast.
LanguageData.DO_Yeast_Desc: Active yeast cultivated from flour. Used to ferment dough for softer bread.
LanguageData.DO_UnfermentedDough_Desc: Simple dough mixed from flour. It can be baked into hard travel bread.
LanguageData.DO_FermentedDough_Desc: Dough proofed with yeast. Requires more preparation, but yields much better bread.
LanguageData.DO_Puls_Desc: A thin porridge boiled from cracked wheat. Cheap and filling, but not pleasant.
LanguageData.DO_MilitarisBread_Desc: Dense, dry field bread made from unfermented dough. Long-lasting and practical.
LanguageData.DO_LeavenedBread_Desc: Soft white bread baked from fermented dough. Time-intensive to produce, but exceptionally nourishing and satisfying.
LanguageData.DO_FermentationBucket_Desc: A sealed bucket setup used to culture wheat yeast from flour.
LanguageData.DO_Oven_Desc: A dedicated oven that keeps steady heat for high-quality leavened bread.
LanguageData.DO_Recipe_MakeFlour_Desc: Process wheat ears into flour at the agronomy bench.
LanguageData.DO_Recipe_MakeYeastBulk_Desc: Prepare a larger batch of yeast for sustained baking.
LanguageData.DO_Recipe_MakeUnfermentedDough_Desc: Mix flour into basic dough for hard bread.
LanguageData.DO_Recipe_MakeFermentedDough_Desc: Add yeast while mixing dough to prepare high-quality bread dough.
LanguageData.DO_Recipe_CookPuls_Desc: Boil wheat into a basic porridge. It is cheap but lowers mood.
LanguageData.DO_Recipe_BakeMilitarisBread_Desc: Bake durable field bread from unfermented dough.
LanguageData.DO_Recipe_BakeLeavenedBread_Desc: Bake soft white bread from fermented dough in a bread oven.
LanguageData.DO_Thought_AtePuls_Desc: It fills the stomach, but the texture is rough and the taste is bland.
LanguageData.DO_Thought_AteMilitarisBread_Desc: This bread is dry and hard. Useful for travel, unpleasant for daily meals.
LanguageData.DO_Thought_AteLeavenedBread_Desc: So soft and fragrant. It feels like my teeth are in heaven.
LanguageData.DO_Hediff_DryMouth_Desc: A lingering dry-mouth feeling after eating dense hard bread. Eating is a bit slower.
LanguageData.DO_Hediff_WorkFocus_Desc: Good fermentation and proper baking leave the body warm and focused, improving work speed for a while.
